flags.concern: Sharing pipeline: direct mode's scheduled media path is not viable if it only downloads external URLs to temp files and enqueues the existing request payload.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — clarified that scheduled direct posts match existing message-based behavior (temp files left for executor, not cleaned up), and that media_hints preserves original URLs for future re-download support; (2) FLAG-002 — added `user_details: {'direct_post': True}` to source_context metadata to satisfy XProvider's guard, since _build_tweet_caption uses provided tweet_text as-is when base_description is set; (3) FLAG-003 — changed direct mode entry condition from "has media_urls" to "no message_link and no message_id", which naturally supports text-only posts, direct retweets (action=retweet + target_post only), and media posts alike.

flags.concern: Social publishing metadata: the proposed direct request shape drops `user_details`, but the X provider currently refuses to publish without it.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — clarified that scheduled direct posts match existing message-based behavior (temp files left for executor, not cleaned up), and that media_hints preserves original URLs for future re-download support; (2) FLAG-002 — added `user_details: {'direct_post': True}` to source_context metadata to satisfy XProvider's guard, since _build_tweet_caption uses provided tweet_text as-is when base_description is set; (3) FLAG-003 — changed direct mode entry condition from "has media_urls" to "no message_link and no message_id", which naturally supports text-only posts, direct retweets (action=retweet + target_post only), and media posts alike.

flags.concern: Admin chat social sharing: the plan narrows direct mode to `media_urls`-driven posts/replies and explicitly errors on retweets, which does not match the requested no-Discord direct mode support for `action` including `retweet`.
flags.evidence: Addressed three significant flags: (1) FLAG-001 — clarified that scheduled direct posts match existing message-based behavior (temp files left for executor, not cleaned up), and that media_hints preserves original URLs for future re-download support; (2) FLAG-002 — added `user_details: {'direct_post': True}` to source_context metadata to satisfy XProvider's guard, since _build_tweet_caption uses provided tweet_text as-is when base_description is set; (3) FLAG-003 — changed direct mode entry condition from "has media_urls" to "no message_link and no message_id", which naturally supports text-only posts, direct retweets (action=retweet + target_post only), and media posts alike.
